meaning: adj.荷马的,荷马时代的,荷马式的
examples: His proposals for the reform of poetry, especially Homeric poetry, represent clearly a radical departure from Greek educational practices and beliefs.
Even in the Homeric poems, we can see that these ideas are transient.
Troy is the name of an archaeological site, the traditional location of Homeric Troy.
The work is heavily Homeric, remote and noble, full of gloomy gray seas and doomy gray mountains, and ribboned with bardlike epithets.
Imagine a cross between a Homeric rhapsode, chanting one of the epics in front of a roaring fire, and the blues singer Muddy Waters.
